title: BAC Retro-Extrapolation
description: Calculate peak blood alcohol concentration with the Widmark formula, then retro-extrapolate a measured BAC backward from the blood-draw time to an earlier event time on a live BAC-over-time graph with a 0.08% legal limit.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
Forensic Toxicology
Blood Alcohol Concentration
Widmark Formula
Retro-extrapolation
bloomLevel: Apply (L3) — calculate BAC with the Widmark formula and perform a retro-extrapolation.

learningObjectives: Calculate peak BAC from weight, sex, and number of drinks using the Widmark formula.
Retro-extrapolate a measured BAC backward to an earlier event time.
Explain why a sub-limit BAC at draw can still indicate over-limit BAC earlier.
educational.prerequisites: Linear rates; reading a line graph; basic algebra

pedagogical.interactionPattern: Calculator + graph: set inputs, drag the event-time marker, press Calculate to reveal the retro-extrapolation arithmetic
pedagogical.assessment: Students compute BAC at the event time by hand and verify against the revealed result
pedagogical.modelType: Widmark peak BAC with linear zero-order elimination and optional absorption phase
controls: drag the event-time marker on the graph
